observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive implement the following:
10) Develop a ten (10)-year Solid Waste Management Plan containing, but not limited to, the components listed in Section 17 of Republic Act No. 9003 in coordination with various sectors within the locality; and
11) Conduct information and educational campaign at the barangay level with emphasis on their responsibility on solid waste segregation.

We recommended to the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit the List of PPAs for CY 2019 to COA and its quarterly monitoring report, and to strictly observe the guidelines set forth by the Commission to promote transparency and good governance, encourage public participation and right to information.

We recommended that Management, particularly the members of the BAC, to strictly comply with the aforesaid regulations. In case/s of procurement method applied not within the approved Annual Procurement Plan, the BAC through a resolution must recommend to the Head of Procuring Agency the specific alternative method of procurement to be used and secure the prior approval, before resorting to any alternative method of procurement pursuant to Sec. 48.1 and 48.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
We recommended that Management require the accounting office to submit SB Resolution and other necessary documents to support the claim as evidence and proof of its legality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all concerned officials to prepare all necessary records pertaining to the Trust Fund and the necessary adjustments for the transfer of PPE Accounts to the General Fund books.

1.8 We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to continue the conduct of
complete physical count on all types of PPE, and prepare the
prescribed annual RPCPPE thereon which shall be submitted to
the Accounting Office for reconciliation with the subsidiary
accounts and balances to ensure reliability of PPE account
balances; and
b. Henceforth, submit the RPCPPE for the year ended December 31,
2021 to the Audit Team for further review.

2.6 We recommended that Local Chief Executive enjoin the:
a. Municipal Accountant record all purchases of supplies and
materials following the perpetual inventory method and an
inventory control account should be maintained for each inventory
item pursuant to Section 114 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume I; and
b. The Municipal GSO to maintain stock cards, consolidate weekly the
RIS using the SSMI to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant,
who shall compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory
using the moving average method as provided under Section 115 of
the same mentioned manual and prepare JEV to record the
appropriate expense accounts.

3.7 We recommended that Management ensure due notification be made
pertaining to the time and date of every scheduled delivery of goods,
materials and services to the Auditor(cid:182)s Office regularly pursuant to
Section 6.6 of COA Circular No. 95-006.

4.4 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor:
a. Require the MPDO to submit annually (at the beginning of the
year) a list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to
be implemented during the year which shall include the project
name, implementing unit, office or division if not the agency as a
whole, brief description of the PPA, contractor or supplier, if any,
mode of procurement, funding source, cost or approved budget,
project duration including start and completion dates, and
location in compliance with item 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-
004;
b. Inform the Office of the SA and/or the ATL within ten (10) days
after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of
the program/activity that appropriate project signboards and/or
public notices are already posted pursuant to item 3.1 of the same
COA Circular; and
c. Submit to the ATL monthly monitoring report of the PPAs and
faithfully follow the provisions COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated
January 30, 2013, on the Information and Publicity of PPAs of
Government Agencies.

5.5 We recommended that Management ensure that a copy of contract
(such as MOA, purchase orders and its pertinent supporting
documents) be submitted for contract review within the prescribed five
(5)-day period from the execution / issuance of the contract pursuant to
COA Circular 2009-001.

6.4 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant
and MDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on LDRRMF every
month, whether there is transaction or utilization thereof or none for
the period, as required by COA Circular No. 2012-002.

7.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant immediately comply with the
requirements set forth under Section 100 of PD No. 1445 relative to the
timely submission of financial records, reports and disbursement
vouchers in order to have a reliable basis in the verification and
analysis of the financial transactions of the agency.

We recommended that the Honorable Municipal Mayor:
1) Create an Inventory Team to conduct physical count of PPE and submit to the Municipal Accountant the result/report hereof, copy furnish the Auditor; and
2) Direct the GSO and Municipal Accountant to faithfully maintain Property Cards and PPELC for proper recording and monitoring of properties of the LGU.

We recommended that the Honorable Municipal Mayor implement the following:
3) Direct the MDRRMO, LDC, and Accounting Office to immediately furnish the Audit Team the following: Local Sanggunian Resolution on the use of QRF, LDRRMF Investment Plan and its supplemental plan, Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and Utilization Report of Transferred Funds from the Granter LGU; and
4) Direct the Accounting and Budget Officer to maintain registries for recording and ultimately in the presentation of corresponding expense accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Honorable Municipal Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee, consisting of the Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal Treasurer and the MPDO to submit the Approved Local Ordinance/ Supplemental Budget for the BGCM Fund and Monthly Report on Fund Utilization and Status of PPAs Implementation and copy of posting of reports and written notice and comply with the maintenance of separate records for the accounting and budgeting of the said fund.

We recommended that Management implement the following:
6) Faithfully comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and the existing rules and regulations on the proper cash management and reporting;
7) Direct the Treasurer to complete the details in recording transactions in Cashbooks; and
8) Establish a sound internal control by issuing checks and recording them in chronological sequence in the Report of Checks Issued and Cashbooks in accordance to Section 42, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GSO and Municipal Accountant to faithfully maintain Property Cards, PPELC and RPLC for recording and monitoring of properties of the LGU.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
12) Direct the Accountant to refrain from granting cash advances without full liquidation of the previous cash advances granted, and
13) Submit to the Office of the Auditor the liquidation reports within ten (10) days after the ensuing month of the transactions.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
15) Direct the Municipal Treasurer, Accountant, Budget Officer, MPDC, GSO and GAD Focal Person, to immediately comply and submit copies of deficiencies noted in the Validation Checklist to the Office of the Team Leader for review, and
16) Direct the same to faithfully accomplish and maintain financial records of all transactions every month.

We recommended that the Management:
17) Re-evaluate policy on the hiring of contractual worker;
18) Reassess capability of the existing human resource; and
19) Discontinue contracts of service which might be found unnecessary, and strictly comply with the requisites of law on the procurement of manpower.

We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Office or the Inventory Committee to:
21) Mobilize additional competent personnel to facilitate the inventory of all PPE items; and
22) Strengthen the controls on the procedures of PPE handling and recording by performing a periodic reconciliation between the records of the accounting office and the actual property and inventory results.

We recommended that Management conduct inventory of its road networks system and record in the books of accounts its equivalent amount pursuant to Item IX of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

We recommended that Management:
25) Create Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP);
26) Develop Long-Term LDRRMF Plan which will serve as guide in building the calamity preparedness and adaptive capacity in terms of disasters; and
27) Establish Incident Command System (ICS) to ensure effective consequences management of disaster as explicitly provided in RA 10121.
1. Create an Inventory Team to hasten the conduct of physical count of PPE and submit to the Municipal Accountant the result/report hereof, copy furnish the Auditor, and

3. Develop a 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan containing, but not limited to, the components listed in Section 17 of Republic Act No. 9003 in coordination with the various sectors within the locality;
5. Conduct information and educational campaign at the barangay level with emphasis on their responsibility on solid waste segregation.
7. Direct the Accountant to refrain from granting cash advances without full liquidation of the previous cash advances;
8. Submit to the Office of the Auditor the liquidation reports within 10 days after the ensuing month of the transactions.

10. We recommended to the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit the PPAs for CY 2019 to COA and its quarterly monitoring report, and to strictly observe the guidelines set forth by the Commission to promote good governance, encourage public participation and right to information.

18. We recommended that Management, particularly the members of the BAC, to strictly comply with the aforesaid regulations. In case/s of procurement method applied not within the approved APP, the BAC through a resolution must recommend to the Head of Procuring Agency the specific alternative method of procurement to be used and secure the prior approval, before resorting to any alternative method of procurement pursuant to Sec. 48.1 and 48.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
20. Mobilize additional competent personnel to facilitate the inventory of all PPE items;

21. Strengthen the controls on the procedures of PPE handling and recording by performing a periodic reconciliation between the records of the accounting office and the actual property and inventory results.
22. We recommended that Management require the accounting office to submit SB Resolution and other necessary documents to support the claim as evidence and proof of its legality.
29. Create Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP);
31. Establish Incident Command System (ICS) to ensure effective consequences management of disaster as explicitly provided in R.A. 10121.

32. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all concerned officials to prepare all necessary records pertaining to the Trust Fund and the necessary adjustments for the transfer of PPE Accounts to the General Fund books.

1.7 We recommended that the Honorable Municipal Mayor:
a. Create an Inventory Team to conduct physical count of PPE and
submit to the Municipal Accountant the result/report hereof, copy
furnish the Auditor; and
b. Direct the GSO and Municipal Accountant to strictly maintain
Property Cards, PPELC and RPLC for proper recording and
monitoring of properties of the LGU.

2.9 We recommended that the Honorable Municipal Mayor implement
the following:
a. Direct the Municipal DRRMO, LDC, and Accounting Office to
immediately furnish the Audit Team the following: Local
Sanggunian Resolution on the use of QRF, LDRRMF Investment
Plan and its supplemental plan, Monthly Report on Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF and Utilization Report of Transferred
Funds from the Granter LGU; and
b. Direct the Accounting and Budget Officer to maintain registries
for recording and ultimately in the presentation of corresponding
expense accounts in the Financial Statements.

3.13 We recommended that the Honorable Municipal Mayor direct the
Local Finance Committee, consisting of the Municipal Budget Officer,
the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Planning and
Development Officer (MPDO) to submit the Approved Local
Ordinance/Supplemental Budget for the BGCM Fund and Monthly
Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project/Activity
Implementation and copy of posting of reports and written notice and
comply with the maintenance of separate records for the accounting
and budgeting of the said fund.

4.15 We recommended that Management implement the following:
a. Faithfully comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002
dated February 10, 1997 and the existing rules and regulations on
the proper cash management and reporting;
b. Direct the Treasurer to complete the details in recording
transactions in the Cashbooks; and
c. Establish a sound internal control by issuing checks and recording
them in chronological sequence in the Report of Checks Issued
and Cashbooks in accordance to Section 42, Volume I of the
NGAS Manual for LGUs.

5.7 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant immediately comply with the requirements set forth
under Section 100 of P.D. No. 1445 relative to the timely submission of
financial records, reports and disbursement vouchers in order to have
a reliable basis in the verification and analysis of the financial
transactions of the agency.
2. Direct the GSO and Municipal Accountant faithfully maintain Property Cards, PPELC and RPLC for recording and monitoring of properties of the LGU.

4. Create a Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to conduct regular monitoring on the implementation of the Solid Waste Management Plan for the early resolution of the problems, issues and concerns encountered during its implementation;
6. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately liquidate cash advances as soon as the purpose has been served;

9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the RCD, along with the duplicate of issued ORs and its supporting documents. We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit the RAAF to the Audit Team for proper monitoring of the accountable forms. Further, the Municipal Accountant, on the basis of the RCDs as submitted by the Municipal Treasurer shall record the collections.

11. We recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the Monthly Reports on the Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF from January to December 2019 and the LDRRMF Investment Plan, in support to the disbursements made from the same.

12. Direct the Municipal Treasurer, Accountant, Budget Officer, MPDC, GSO and GAD Focal Person, to immediately comply and submit copies of deficiencies noted in the Validation Checklist to the Office of the Team Leader for review; and
13. Direct the same to strictly accomplish and maintain financial records of all transactions every month.

14. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC), the annual LDRRMF Investment Plan which shall present the 30% allocation for Quick Response Fund in lump-sum and the 70% allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded pursuant to Section 12(c) of the PDRRMA and COA Circular No. 2012-002.
15. Re-evaluate policy on the hiring of contractual worker;
16. Reassess capability of the existing human resource;
17. Discontinue contracts of service which might be found unnecessary, and strictly comply with the requisites of law on the procurement of manpower.

19. We recommended that Management direct the concerned officials to submit copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and AIR on time to the Auditor’s Office and notify the said office of the delivery of procured items within 24 hours from such acceptance of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries as required under Section A (1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
23. Direct the Municipal Accountant to get the monthly bank statements promptly and regularly;
24. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements of all bank accounts together with the bank statements and paid checks to the auditor;
25. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile his cashbooks entries with that from the accounting records at least quarterly.

26. We recommended that Management conduct inventory of its road networks system and record in the books of accounts its equivalent amount pursuant to Item IX of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015

27. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned official or the Municipal Assessor to give emphasis on the collection of delinquent real property taxpayers by applying the remedies in collection of delinquent taxes as embodied in Section 254 of R.A. 7160 in order to set up in the books the estimated real property tax collectibles and the special education fund share.
28. We recommended that the Management allocate 1% of IRA for establishment of LCPC and implementation of their programs.
30. Develop Long-Term LDRRMF Plan which will serve as guide in building the calamity preparedness and adaptive capacity in terms of disasters;

We recommended that Management, particularly the members of the BAC, to strictly comply with the aforesaid regulations. In case/s of procurement method applied not within the approved APP, the BAC through a resolution must recommend to the Head of Procuring Agency the specific alternative method of procurement to be used and secure the prior approval, before resorting to any alternative method of procurement pursuant to Sec. 48.1 and 48.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned officials to submit copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and AIR on time to the Auditor’s Office and notify the said office of the delivery of the procured items within 24 hours from such acceptance of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries as required under Section A (1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
Prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards;

Strengthen the controls on the procedures of PPE handling and recording by performing a periodic reconciliation between the records of the accounting office and the actual property and inventory results.
We recommended that Management require the accounting office to submit SB Resolution and other necessary documents to support the claim as evidence and proof of its legality.
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements of all bank accounts together with the bank statements and paid checks to the auditor;
Create Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP);
Establish Incident Command System (ICS) to ensure effective consequences management of disaster as explicitly provided in R.A. 10121.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all concerned officials to prepare all necessary records pertaining to the Trust Fund and the necessary adjustments for the transfer of PPE Accounts to General Fund books.

1.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Create an Inventory Team to hasten the conduct of physical count of PPE
and submit to the Municipal Accountant the result/report hereof, copy furnish
the Auditor, and
b) Direct the GSO and Municipal Accountant to faithfully maintain Property
Cards, PPELC and RPLC for proper recording and monitoring of properties of
the LGU.

2.4 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Develop a ten (10)-year Solid Waste Management Plan
containing, but not limited to, the components listed in Section
17 of Republic Act No. 9003 in coordination with the various
sectors within the locality;
b) Create a Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to conduct
regular monitoring on the implementation of the Solid Waste
Management Plan for the early resolution of the problems, issues
and concerns encountered during its implementation, and
c) Conduct information and educational campaign at the barangay
level with emphasis on their responsibility on solid waste
segregation.

3.3 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately liquidate cash
advances as soon as the purpose has been served;
b) Direct the Accountant to refrain from granting cash advances
without full liquidation of the previous cash advances granted;
and
c) Submit to the Office of the Auditor the liquidation reports within
ten (10) days after the ensuing month of the transactions.

4.5 We recommended that the Management:
a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately prepare and submit
the RCD, along with the duplicate of issued ORs and its supporting
documents.
b) Request the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the RAAF
to the Audit Team for proper monitoring of the accountable forms.
c) Direct the Municipal Accountant, on the basis of the RCDs as
submitted by the Municipal Treasurer, to record the collections in
the Cash Receipt Journal and prepare a Journal Entry Voucher.

5.4 We recommended that Management direct the MPDC and the Municipal
Engineer to prepare and submit the List of PPAs for CY 2019 to COA
and its quarterly monitoring report, and to faithfully observe the
guidelines set forth by the Commission to promote good governance,
encourage public participation and right to information.

6.7 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant
in coordination with the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Officer to
submit the Monthly Reports on the Receipt and Utilization of the
DRRMF from January to December 2019 and the LDRRMF Investment
Plan, in support to the disbursements made from the same.

7.7 We strongly recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer, Accountant, Budget Officer, MPDC,
GSO and GAD Focal Person, to immediately comply and submit
copies of deficiencies noted in the Validation Checklist to the Office
of the Team Leader for review, and
b. Direct the same to faithfully accomplish and maintain financial
records of all transactions every month.

Management require the Investment Plant which shall present the 30% allocation for Quick Response Fund in lump-sum and the 70% allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded pursuant to Section 12(c) of the PDRRMA and COA Circular No. 2012-002.
Re-evaluate policy on the hiring of contractual worker;
Reassess capability of the existing human resource,
Discontinue contracts of service which might be found unnecessary, and strictly comply with the requisites of law on the procurement of manpower.

We recommended that Management continue promoting a gender responsive governance to comply with the government’s objective of promoting and protecting women’s human rights and economic empowerment and to pass a GAD Code to easily determine the PAPs that addresses gender issues and concerns in compliance with Section 37B Rule VI of Republic Act 9710.

We recommended that the Management identify projects and programs under this fund in accordance with the provision of the DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2005, to provide desirable socio-economic development which are beneficial to the constituents and direct the concerned officials to submit the complete necessary documents for audit.
Mobilize additional competent personnel to facilitate the inventory of all PPE items;
Classify properly PPE items;

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to record purchases of supplies and the like as Inventory Account and record the related expenses account upon issuance of inventories based on RSMI.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to get the monthly bank statements promptly and regularly;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile his cashbooks entries with that from the accounting records at least quarterly.

We recommended that Management conduct inventory of its road networks system and record in the books of accounts its equivalent amount pursuant to Item IX of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned official or the Municipal Assessor to give emphasis on the collection of taxes delinquent real property taxpayers by applying the remedies in collection of delinquent taxes as embodied in Section 254 of R.A. 7160 in order to set up in the books the estimated real property tax collectibles and the special education fund share.
We recommended that the Management allocate 1% of IRA for the establishment of LCPC and implementation of their programs.
Develop Long-Term LDRRMF Plan which will serve as guide in building the calamity preparedness and adaptive capacity in terms of disasters;

We recommended
that the Local Chief
Executive direct all
concerned officials
to prepare all
necessary records
pertaining to the
Trust Fund and the
necessary
adjustments for the
transfer of Property,
Plant and
Equipment
accounts from
Trust Fund books to
General Fund
books.

We recommended
that the Management
widen utilization of
20% development
fund to the three
priority programs
and projects such as
social development,
economic development
and environment
management.

1.7. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer to prepare and submit to the
Sangguniang Bayan, through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction
Management Council (MDRRMC), the annual Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) which
shall present the 30 percent allocation for Quick Response Fund in lump-
sum and the 70 percent allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and
preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded
pursuant to Section 12 (c) of the PDRRMA and COA Circular No. 2012-
002.

2.6. We recommended that Management:
1. Re-evaluate policy on the hiring of contractual worker.
2. Reassess capability of the existing human resource.
3. Discontinue contracts of service which might be found
unnecessary, and strictly comply with the requisites of law on the
procurement of manpower.

3.5. We recommended that the Management, particularly the members of the
Bids and Award Committee, strictly comply with the aforesaid
regulations. In case/s of procurement method applied not within the
approved APP, the BAC through a resolution must recommend to the
Head of Procuring Agency the specific alternative method of procurement
to be used and secure the prior approval, before resorting to any
alternative method of procurement pursuant to Sec. 48.1 and 48.3 of the
revised IRR of RA 9184.

4.4. We recommended that Management:
a. Submit to the Audit Team the list of all on-going government
Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) and those that are to be
implemented during the year.
b. Inform this office within ten (10) days after the award of the
infrastructure project or before the start of the
Programs/Projects/ Activities that the appropriate project
signboards and/or public notices are already posted, and
c. Submit to this Office the monthly monitoring report on PPAs.

5.4. We recommended that Management direct the concerned officials to
submit copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and Acceptance and
Inspection Reports on time to the Auditor’s Office and notify the said
office of the delivery of procured items within twenty-four (24) hours
from such acceptance of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries as
required under Section A (1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section
118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

6.5. We recommended that Management continue promoting a gender
responsive governance to comply with the government’s objective of
promoting and protecting women’s human rights and economic
empowerment and to pass a GAD Code to easily determine the PAPs that
addresses gender issues and concerns in compliance with Section 37B
Rule VI of Republic Act No. 9710.

7.4. We recommended that Management require the accountant, the
Treasurer and other concerned officials to strictly comply with the
reporting procedures and deadlines of the submission to the Auditor
office.

8.4. We recommended that the Management identify projects and programs
under this fund in accordance with the provision of the DILG and DBM
Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2005, to provide desirable socio-economic
development which are beneficial to the constituents and direct the
concerned officials to submit the complete required documents necessary
for audit.

9.3. We recommended that the Management maximize the utilization of the
Materials Recovery Facility (MRF). We also recommend that
management assist the barangays technically or in any other manner
deemed necessary in order to sustain the operation of the MRFs.

11.5. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
record purchases of supplies and the like as Inventory account and record
the related expenses account upon issuance of inventories bases on RSMI.

12.3. We recommended that Management require the accounting office to
submit SB Resoultion and other necessary documents to suport the claim
as evidence and proof of its legality.

We recommended that Management;
Direct the
Municipal
Accountant to
get the
monthly bank
statements
promptly and
regularly.
2. Require the
Municipal
Accountant to
prepare and
submit Bank
Reconciliation
Statements.

1. Strict compliance with
the Laws, Rules and      
Regulations of LDRRMF    
implementation is hereby 
required, and Submit     
Accomplishment Report    
thereof to the Auditor   
concerned for proper

2. The Municipal         
Accountant should set    
aside a subsidy to other 
funds and transfer prior 
years unexpended and or

the LDRRMF equivalent to 
30% from the General Fund
to the Trust Book using  
Trust Liability – DRRM.   
3. The Municipal Budget  
Officer should likewise  
maintain subsidiary      
ledger and records of the
appropriation            
corresponding            
expenditures and balances
of the LDRRMF for the    
current and previous     
years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management;


        1.  Direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  get  the  monthly  bank
           statements promptly and regularly.


        2. Require the Municipal Accountant  to  prepare  and  submit  Bank
           Reconciliation Statements of all bank accounts together with the
           bank statements and paid checks to the Auditor


        3. Direct  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to  reconcile  his  cashbooks
           entries  with  that  from  the  accounting  records   at   least
           quarterly.


      Management concurred with our recommendations and committed to  comply
      with the above provision of rules and regulations.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to;
       1. Maintain an updated cashbook on a daily basis, by fund and  or  by
          account.
       2. Prepare and submit Report of Collection and  Deposit  (RCD),  with
          all its supporting documents within the reglementary period set by
          rules.

      Management concurred with our recommendations and committed to  comply
      with the above provision of rules and regulations.

We recommended the following measures, to wit;

   a. If no records could be found, start recording the actual existence  of
      equipment,  furniture  and  fixtures  by  office   and   if   possible
      reconstruct the records.
   b. Instruct the General Services Officer to provide  property  number  of
      all properties, at the  same  time,  maintain  stock  cards  with  the
      Municipal Accountant who will maintain a subsidiary ledger card.
   c. The Municipal Accountant should prepare necessary adjustments  on  the
      deficiencies  noted  in  the  course  of  inventory  taking  and  make
      reconciliation with  inventory  report,  stock  cards  and  subsidiary
      ledgers.
   Management concurred with our recommendations  and  committed  to  comply
   with the above provision of rules and regulations.

We recommended that  Management conduct inventory of its road networks
system and record in the books of accounts its  equivalent  amount  pursuant
to item IX of COA Circular 2015-008 date November 23, 2015.

Management concurred with our recommendation and committed  to  comply  with
the above provision of rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE)  require  the  concerned
official or the Municipal Assessor to give emphasis  on  the  collection  of
taxes and or delinquent real property taxes by applying the remedies in  the
collection of delinquent taxes as embodied in Section 254 of R.A.  No.  7160
in  order  to  set  up  in  the  books  the  estimated  real  property   tax
collectibles and the special education fund share.


Management concurred with our recommendation and committed  to  comply  with
the above provision of rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management allocate one percent  (1%)  of  IRA
for the establishment of  Local  Council  for  the  Protection  of  Children
(LCPC) and the implementation of their programs.

Management concurred with our recommendation and committed  to  comply  with
the above provision of rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management:


   1. Create  Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP)


        2. Develop Long Term LDRRMF Plan  which  will  serve  as  guide  in
           building the calamity  preparedness  and  adaptive  capacity  in
           terms of disasters.


        3. Establish Incident Command  System  (ICS)  to  ensure  effective
           consequences management of disaster as  explicitly  provided  in
           R.A. No. 10121.

Management concurred with our recommendations and committed to  comply  with
the above provision of rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management:


       a. Direct the Board for the preparation of the Solid Waste Management
           Plan which should be reviewed and updated as required by  law  to
          ensure its sustainability, relevance and effectiveness;


       b.   Collaborate   the  campaign  down  to  the  barangay   officials
          concerned;

Management concurred with our recommendations and committed to  comply  with
the above provision of rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive


       1. Allocate for 5% Calamity Fund which should  be  transferred  to  a
          separate account special trust fund and utilized solely for LDRRMF
          purpose activities


       2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit report  on  the  sources
          and utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis,


       3. Prepare and submit Annual  DRRM  program  with  specific  for  the
          seventy percent (70%) preparedness and thirty percent (30%)  quick
          response.
   Management concurred with our recommendations  and  committed  to  comply
   with the above provision of rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  direct  all  concerned
officials to prepare all necessary records pertaining to the Trust Fund  and
the necessary adjustments for the transfer of Property, Plant and  Equipment
accounts from Trust Fund books to General Fund books.


      Management concurred with our recommendation and committed  to  comply
with the above provision of rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management widen the allocation of utilization
of 20% development fund to the three priority programs and projects such  as
social development, economic development and environment management.

      Management concurred with our recommendation and committed  to  comply
with the above provision of rules and regulations.

It is recommended that   
the top management should
initiate necessary       
reforms for fiscal       
reporting and            
accountability by:       
Demonstrating leadership 
by example;              
Training personnel to    
gain work competence     
and/or hiring additional 
qualified personnel and  
or restricting existing  
person; and              
Enforcing due compliance 
with laws, rules and     
regulations on the       
rendition of financial   
reports.                 

It is further recommended
that the top management  
should direct the        
concerned municipal      
accountable officer to   
submit promptly the      
periodic financial       
reports together with the
pertinent supporting     
documents to the         
concerned local auditor  
so that timely post-audit
of transactions could be 
made and verification of 
the same could be        
facilitated.             

Consequently, the top    
management could also be 
informed immediately of  
any deficiencies/errors  
detected and             
corrections/adjustments  
thereof could be effected
to their advantage.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:

It is recommended that the top management should initiate necessary reforms for fiscal
reporting and accountability by:

a. Demonstrating leadership by example;

b, Training personnel to gain work competence and’ or hiring additional qualified
personnel giving the right job for the right person; and

¢ Enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the rendition of
financial reports.

It is further recommended that the top management should direct the concerned
municipal accountable officer to submit prompily the periodic financial reports together with the
pertinent supporting documents to the concerned local auditor so that timely post-audit of
transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated. Consequently, the
top management could also be informed immediately of any deficiencies’errors detected and
corrections'adjustments thereof could be effected to their advantage.

Recommendation/s:

It is recommend that the management should widen the allocation of utilization of 20%
development fund to the three priority programs and projects such as social development,
economic development and environment management.

Recommendation/s:

1. Strict compliance with the Laws, Rules and Regulations of LORRMF implementation is hereby
required, and Submit Accomplishment Report thereof to the Auditor concerned for proper
evaluation.

2. The Municipal Accountant should set aside a subsidy to other funds and transfer prior years
unexpended and or unobligated balance of the LDRRMF equivalent to 30% from the General
Fund to the Trust Book using Trust Liability - DRRM.

3. The Municipal Budget Officer should likewise maintain subsidiary ledger and records of the
appropriation corresponding expenditures and balances of the LDRRMF for the current and
previous years.

Recommendation/s:

*Prioritize the mitiation of physical count on all PPE as this will help concerned officials in the
proper discharge of their duties and responsibilities towards fair presentation of the agency’s
Sfinancial statements.

* Create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and submit
carresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately thereafier.

* The Accounting office and General Services office I reasury office shall likewise, maintain and

update an Equipment ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively and ofientimes reconcile
balances of each cards maintained by them and with the balances in the Report on Physical
Count to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.

* Provision for depreciation shall be accurately provided on all depreciable assets.

Recommendation/s:

Require the concerned Municipal Accountant to observe the keeping of accounts and
subsidiary records of all accounts in compliance with Section 11 and 114 (2) of Prestdential
Decree No. 1445. Furthermore, direct him to conduct a thorough inquiry and verification on the
circumstances surrounding the existence of those dormant accounts, and their proper disposition
as prescribed by applicable COA regulations.

Recommendation/s:
Encourage and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid
waste management system and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM)
Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447,

Recommendation/s:

We recommend that the management observe/adhere 10 the guidelines stipulated in COA
Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 RE: Information and Publicity of Programs,
Projects and Activities of government agencies. This is 1o promote Good Governance through
transparency and accountability.

Recommendation/s:

1. Management should formulate Plans and Programs for GAD activities.
2. Management should submit GAD Accomplishment Report to the auditor concerned,

the physical inventory of its properties, plant and equipment and submit the related Report of the Physical Count on the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) using the prescribed format to the Auditor for Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume 1.

We  recommend Municipal Account be directed to make the adjustments in the books of accounts for the transfer of the subject PPE accounts from the Trust Fund books to the General Fund, take up the unrecognized Income from Donations and provide the corresponding depreciation_expenses_Prepare the related  Journal  Entry Voucher and submit the same to the Auditor for audit.

We reco! and management agreed to strictly with the provision of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 specifically on the reporting for the LDRRMF. The Municipal Accountant should be directed to set up a special trust fund account for any unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to properly track the utilization of the fund.

We recommend that management  should initiate measures designed to come up with a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the GSIS  defining therein  the agreed balance of payables to GSIS, if any, and the terms and conditions in the settlement thereof. The following steps may help in facilitating reconciliation of records,

& Secure detailed profile of payables per GSIS records;

b. Reconstruct old GSIS

sccounts, if necessary,
Rej
